case.title: BROWN-FORMAN DISTILLERS CORP. v. COLLECTOR OF REVENUE

opinion.h1: BROWN-FORMAN DISTILLERS CORP. v. COLLECTOR OF REVENUE, 359 U.S. 28 (1959)
opinion.h4: BROWN-FORMAN DISTILLERS CORP. v. COLLECTOR OF REVENUE. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF LOUISIANA. No. 142. Decided March 2, 1959.
opinion.p: The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for certiorari, certiorari is denied.
opinion.h4: E T & W N C TRANSPORTATION CO. v. CURRIE, 359 U.S. 28 (1959)
opinion.p: 359 U.S. 28 E T & W N C TRANSPORTATION CO. v. CURRIE, COMMISSIONER OF REVENUE OF NORTH CAROLINA. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF NORTH CAROLINA. No. 524. Decided March 2, 1959.
Kester Walton and James H. Epps, Jr. for appellant.
The judgment is affirmed. Northwestern States Portland Cement Co. v. Minnesota, and Williams v. Stockham Valves & Fittings, Inc., 358 U.S. 450.
MR. JUSTICE FRANKFURTER, MR. JUSTICE WHITTAKER and MR. JUSTICE STEWART dissent for the reasons stated in the dissenting opinions in these cases.
